title: Ranunculus mazzettii
taxonomy: Ranunculus mazzettii is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. This family is commonly known as the buttercup or crowfoot family. Within the Ranunculaceae family, Ranunculus mazzettii is classified as part of the genus Ranunculus. The genus Ranunculus is a large and diverse group of plants that are often found in moist environments. The classification of Ranunculus mazzettii reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Ranunculaceae family.

intro: Ranunculus mazzettii, also known as Mazzetti's buttercup, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and edible roots. The roots of Ranunculus mazzettii have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and isoflavones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly used to alleviate respiratory symptoms and reduce inflammation.
